turns.text: Here's a close-up of my tomato plant. Can you describe the leaves?
turns.image_description: Close-up of healthy tomato plant leaves, bright green color, morning dew visible, uniform leaf size

turns.text: The leaves appear healthy with vibrant green coloration and uniform size. No signs of disease or damage visible.
I took another photo today. Notice the yellowing edges on some leaves.
turns.image_description: Same plant with two leaves showing yellowing edges, sunlit from the left, no visible insects

turns.text: I see the marginal chlorosis on two leaves—this could indicate early-stage nutrient deficiency or disease. Would you like me to check for other symptoms?
Here's the latest update. The condition seems worse now.
turns.image_description: Plant with three leaves showing brown spots, partial wilting, overcast lighting

turns.text: The brown spots and wilting suggest the disease is progressing. I notice new growth at the base, but the older leaves are severely affected. What's your concern now?
One last photo. What's the final status of the plant?
turns.image_description: Plant with all leaves browned and fallen, new shoots visible, even lighting

turns.text:
What is the final condition of the tomato plant?

B) Plant has fully died with all leaves browned and fallen
A) Plant showing initial yellowing symptoms
C) Plant with new shoots growing from the base
D) Plant in early stage of nutrient deficiency

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 established baseline health. Turn 2 identified initial yellowing as early symptoms. Turn 3 showed worsening with brown spots and wilting. Turn 5 confirmed complete leaf browning and mortality despite new shoots.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: Progressive disease stages must be tracked to determine mortality
